title: Classification note
body: This platform preserves the brief’s exact aggregate counts. The brief itself distinguishes between standalone law and robust multi-layered frameworks in a few countries, but also groups five jurisdictions into a top-tier binding or enacted category for summary purposes.

title: Scope note
body: The article refers to both 54 AU member states in the state-of-play section and 54 countries in the executive summary. The interface follows the headline figure of 54 AU states reviewed and flags the inconsistency here rather than smoothing it away.

title: Map note
body: The map classifies only the countries explicitly named in the brief text. The brief also refers to a companion country-by-country mapping, but that source is not included here, so unnamed jurisdictions remain inside the brief’s aggregate totals.

title: Source note
body: All content on this page is drawn from the supplied DIID policy brief and the examples it names directly. No additional country classifications have been added beyond those stated in the brief.
